message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="All 5 polish tasks complete, CI green">
ALL POLISH TASKS COMPLETE! Every task is committed, pushed, and CI green.

Final status:
- Task #1: a11y error elements -- DONE (9cdc020)
- Task #2: Forgot password link -- DONE (6fc51ca)
- Task #3: HTMX progressive enhancement -- DONE (a68f9a2)
- Task #4: aria-live announcements -- DONE (554c928)
- Task #5: Password visibility toggle -- DONE (649fb5d)

The todo list app now has:
- All MVP features (US-1 through US-8) working
- HTMX progressive enhancement (works without JS too)
- Proper a11y: always-in-DOM errors, aria-live announcements, password toggle
- Forgot password placeholder page
- All tests passing, CI green

Great work, team. I'm going to report to the project owner and begin shutdown. Stand by for shutdown requests.
</teammate-message>

content.text: All five polish tasks are complete and CI is green. Let me pull the final state and do one last UX review before we wrap up.

content.text: Final state looks good. Let me send my wrap-up to the team lead.

input.content:
Final UX review complete. All five polish tasks are solid. Here's my summary:

**Overall UX Assessment -- Ready to Ship**

The app passes what I call the "Trunk Test" -- a first-time user landing on any page can immediately answer:
1. What site is this? (Todo List -- clear from the title)
2. What page am I on? (Clear h1 on every page)
3. What are the major sections? (Simple layout, no clutter)
4. What can I do here? (Obvious calls to action -- "Sign in", "Create account", "Add todo")
5. How do I get back? (Navigation links, "Back to sign in", "Cancel")

**What works well:**
- Copy is concise everywhere. Labels are 1-2 words. Button labels describe actions. No happy talk.
- Progressive enhancement is solid: everything works without JS, HTMX makes it snappy, password toggle is a nice touch.
- Error handling is user-friendly: errors appear inline near the relevant input, in plain language.
- The rapid-entry pattern for adding todos (input clears, stays focused) respects the user's workflow.
- Two-step delete (details/summary) prevents accidental deletion without a modal dialog.
- Aria-live announcements mean screen reader users get feedback on dynamic changes.

**Minor notes for future iterations (not blocking):**
- The forgot password copy ("try to remember your password") could be slightly more helpful, but it's a placeholder page so it doesn't matter much.
- Inline editing via HTMX (instead of navigating to /edit page) would be a nice future enhancement for the rapid-editing workflow.
- The index/welcome page is bare -- could benefit from sign in/register CTAs for unauthenticated users.

No blocking issues. The MVP is usable, accessible, and doesn't make the user think. Ready for shutdown.
input.summary: Final UX review - all clear, ready for shutdown
